ddls:
CREATE TABLE "provinces" (
  "province_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each province */,
  "province_name" TEXT /* Name of the province */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region to which the province belongs */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the province */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the province record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the province record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("province_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "assessment_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each assessment type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the assessment type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the assessment type */,
  "assessment_format" TEXT /* Format of the assessment (e.g., multiple choice, essay) */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the assessment in minutes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "assessment_scores" (
  "score_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each score entry */,
  "province_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the province where the assessment was conducted */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the assessment */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the type of assessment */,
  "score_value" INTEGER /* Score value obtained in the assessment */,
  "max_score" INTEGER /* Maximum possible score for the assessment */,
  "standard_deviation" REAL /* Standard deviation of scores for the assessment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("score_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_assessment_scores_province_id FOREIGN KEY ("province_id") REFERENCES provinces ("province_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_assessment_scores_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("type_id") REFERENCES assessment_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "schools" (
  "school_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each school */,
  "school_name" TEXT /* Name of the school */,
  "province_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the province where the school is located */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the school */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the school */,
  "school_type" TEXT /* Type of school (e.g., public, private) */,
  "enrollment" INTEGER /* Number of students enrolled in the school */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("school_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_schools_province_id FOREIGN KEY ("province_id") REFERENCES provinces ("province_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "students" (
  "student_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each student */,
  "student_name" TEXT /* Name of the student */,
  "school_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the school the student is enrolled in */,
  "grade" TEXT /* Grade level of the student */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the student */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the student */,
  "english_proficiency" TEXT /* English proficiency level (e.g., native, non-native) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("student_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_students_school_id FOREIGN KEY ("school_id") REFERENCES schools ("school_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "student_assessments" (
  "assessment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each student assessment */,
  "student_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the student who took the assessment */,
  "score_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the score entry for the assessment */,
  "assessment_date" TEXT /* Date the assessment was taken */,
  "result" TEXT /* Result of the assessment (e.g., Pass, Fail) */,
  "teacher_comments" TEXT /* Comments from the teacher */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("assessment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_student_assessments_student_id FOREIGN KEY ("student_id") REFERENCES students ("student_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_student_assessments_score_id FOREIGN KEY ("score_id") REFERENCES assessment_scores ("score_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "teachers" (
  "teacher_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each teacher */,
  "teacher_name" TEXT /* Name of the teacher */,
  "school_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the school the teacher is employed in */,
  "subject" TEXT /* Subject taught by the teacher */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the teacher */,
  "years_of_experience" INTEGER /* Number of years of teaching experience */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("teacher_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_teachers_school_id FOREIGN KEY ("school_id") REFERENCES schools ("school_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "classes" (
  "class_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each class */,
  "class_name" TEXT /* Name of the class */,
  "school_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the school the class is in */,
  "grade" TEXT /* Grade level of the class */,
  "teacher_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the teacher responsible for the class */,
  "class_size" INTEGER /* Number of students in the class */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("class_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_classes_teacher_id FOREIGN KEY ("teacher_id") REFERENCES teachers ("teacher_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_classes_school_id FOREIGN KEY ("school_id") REFERENCES schools ("school_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "class_enrollments" (
  "enrollment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each enrollment */,
  "student_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the student who is enrolled */,
  "class_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the class the student is enrolled in */,
  "enrollment_date" TEXT /* Date of enrollment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("enrollment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_class_enrollments_class_id FOREIGN KEY ("class_id") REFERENCES classes ("class_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_class_enrollments_student_id FOREIGN KEY ("student_id") REFERENCES students ("student_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user's role */,
  "school_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the school the user is associated with (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_school_id FOREIGN KEY ("school_id") REFERENCES schools ("school_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who accessed the system */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date of access */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., login, logout, view, edit) */,
  "resource" TEXT /* Resource accessed (e.g., student data, class data) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
